articles.question: What is the difference between quartz coating, glass coating and ceramic coating?
articles.answer_text: "Quartz", "glass" & "ceramic" are synonyms used for one and same type of product - SiO₂ (silica-dioxide) coatings. Do not get distracted by fake marketing.

articles.question: What is the difference between a quartz coating and a wax?
articles.answer_text: A wax is a layer designed to perform for a limited time, as it doesn't create a permanent or semi-permanent bond with the paint. a wax is a mixture of organic and inorganic components like carnauba wax, beeswax or synthetic PET-C or polymer-based ingredients. It adds gloss and repellency but will fade after few washes due to its low chemical resistance. A ceramic coating, on the other hand, is a mixture of Silica or Polysilazanes with solvents coming in the form of a liquid, which crystallizes after the evaporation of the solvent and creates a solid layer on the surface. This layer is highly chemical resistant and once cured, it can be removed from the surface only by mechanical polishing.
articles.answer_html: A wax is a layer designed to perform for a limited time, as it doesn't create a permanent or semi-permanent bond with the paint. a wax is a mixture of organic and inorganic components like carnauba wax, beeswax or synthetic PET-C or polymer-based ingredients. It adds gloss and repellency but will fade after few washes due to its low chemical resistance. A ceramic coating, on the other hand, is a mixture of Silica or Polysilazanes with solvents coming in the form of a liquid, which crystallizes after the evaporation of the solvent and creates a solid layer on the surface. This layer is highly chemical resistant and once cured, it can be removed from the surface only by mechanical polishing.

articles.question: How to use it?

For waterless wash: Mix Q²M ECOWash in a 15:1 solution. Spray generously onto the surface and into a soft microfiber cloth. Wipe in a straight-line motion. Then, use a dry microfiber towel to buff away any residue.
